req.title: Data retention (negative)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-070 mandates a nightly retention job that purges expired resources and writes a full audit trail. Negative paths cover permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions. The suite targets /cron/retention on fhir-lite. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive — valid expired resources are purged and audit log written; (2) NEG — unauthorized caller is denied with 403 and incident raised; (3) BVA — resource expiring exactly at the retention boundary (midnight cutoff) is purged; (4) STC — resource in a conflicting state (locked/in-use) triggers correct error transition; (5) DT — missing mandatory retention policy element causes policy-mismatch incident. Each test is self-contained and idempotent via setup/teardown helpers.

tests.name: test_retention_job_purges_expired_resources_and_writes_audit
preconditions: At least one resource exists with expiry date in the past
Caller has system-job role with valid CRON_SECRET header
actions: Seed one expired resource via <seed_expired_resource> fixture
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log via <get_audit_entries> helper for the purged resource ID
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Response body contains purged_count >= 1
Expired resource no longer exists (GET /Resource/{id} returns 404 or 410)
Audit log entry exists with action=PURGE and resource_id matching seeded record
trace: R-HLT-070: expired resources shall be purged
R-HLT-070: full audit trail written after purge
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_SECRET = "<CRON_SECRET>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def setup_teardown():
    resource_id = <seed_expired_resource>(BASE_URL)
    yield resource_id
    <cleanup_resource>(BASE_URL, resource_id)

def test_retention_job_purges_expired_resources_and_writes_audit(setup_teardown):
    resource_id = setup_teardown
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {CRON_SECRET}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("purged_count", 0) >= 1

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410)

    audit = <get_audit_entries>(BASE_URL, resource_id)
    assert any(
        e["action"] == "PURGE" and e["resource_id"] == resource_id
        for e in audit
    )

tests.name: test_retention_job_denied_without_valid_credentials
preconditions: Caller presents an invalid or missing Authorization header
Incident management endpoint is reachable via <get_incidents> helper
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query incident log via <get_incidents> helper for policy-mismatch events
expected: HTTP 403 returned
Response body contains error field indicating permission denied
No resources are purged (purged_count absent or 0)
Incident raised with reason=PERMISSION_DENIED in incident log
trace: R-HLT-070: negative path — permission denial must be tested
R-HLT-070: incident raised on policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
INVALID_TOKEN = "<INVALID_TOKEN>"

def test_retention_job_denied_without_valid_credentials():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {INVALID_TOKEN}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body.get("purged_count", 0) == 0

    incidents = <get_incidents>(BASE_URL, reason="PERMISSION_DENIED")
    assert len(incidents) >= 1
    assert incidents[-1]["endpoint"] == "/cron/retention"

tests.name: test_retention_job_purges_resource_expiring_at_exact_boundary
preconditions: A resource exists with expiry timestamp equal to exactly T-0 (now minus 0 seconds)
Caller has system-job role with valid CRON_SECRET header
Server clock is synchronized and known
actions: Seed one resource with expiry set to <boundary_expiry_timestamp> (exactly at retention cutoff) via <seed_resource_with_expiry>
expected: HTTP 200 returned
purged_count >= 1 confirming boundary resource was included
GET /Resource/{id} returns 404 or 410
Audit log entry present for the boundary resource
trace: R-HLT-070: expired resources purged — boundary at exact expiry instant
BVA applied to the retention cutoff timestamp
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import timezone, datetime

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_SECRET = "<CRON_SECRET>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def setup_teardown():
    boundary_ts = <boundary_expiry_timestamp>()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_with_expiry>(BASE_URL, boundary_ts)
    yield resource_id
    <cleanup_resource>(BASE_URL, resource_id)

def test_retention_job_purges_resource_expiring_at_exact_boundary(setup_teardown):
    resource_id = setup_teardown
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {CRON_SECRET}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("purged_count", 0) >= 1

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410)

    audit = <get_audit_entries>(BASE_URL, resource_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "PURGE" for e in audit)

tests.name: test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_conflicting_locked_resource
preconditions: An expired resource exists in LOCKED state (in-use by another process)
Caller has system-job role with valid CRON_SECRET header
State transition from LOCKED to PURGED is not permitted per policy
actions: Seed an expired resource in LOCKED state via <seed_locked_expired_resource>
Query incident log for CONFLICTING_STATE_TRANSITION events
expected: HTTP 207 or 200 returned (partial success acceptable) OR 409 if all resources conflict
Response body includes failed_purges list containing the locked resource ID
Locked resource still exists (GET returns 200 or 423)
Incident raised with reason=CONFLICTING_STATE_TRANSITION for the locked resource
trace: R-HLT-070: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-070: incident raised on policy mismatch
STC: LOCKED -> PURGED transition is invalid and must be blocked
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_SECRET = "<CRON_SECRET>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def setup_teardown():
    resource_id = <seed_locked_expired_resource>(BASE_URL)
    yield resource_id
    <force_unlock_and_cleanup>(BASE_URL, resource_id)

def test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_conflicting_locked_resource(setup_teardown):
    resource_id = setup_teardown
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {CRON_SECRET}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207, 409)

    body = resp.json()
    failed = body.get("failed_purges", [])
    assert resource_id in failed

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code in (200, 423)

    incidents = <get_incidents>(
        BASE_URL, reason="CONFLICTING_STATE_TRANSITION"
    )
    assert any(i["resource_id"] == resource_id for i in incidents)

tests.name: test_retention_job_raises_incident_when_mandatory_policy_element_missing
preconditions: A resource exists that has no retention_policy field (mandatory element absent)
Caller has system-job role with valid CRON_SECRET header
actions: Seed an expired resource with retention_policy field omitted via <seed_resource_no_policy>
Query incident log for MISSING_MANDATORY_ELEMENT events
expected: HTTP 200 or 207 returned
Response body includes policy_errors list referencing the resource with missing policy
Resource is NOT purged (remains accessible)
Incident raised with reason=MISSING_MANDATORY_ELEMENT and resource_id matching seeded record
trace: R-HLT-070: missing mandatory elements must be tested as negative path
R-HLT-070: incident raised on policy mismatch
DT: decision table row — expired=true, policy_present=false => skip purge, raise incident
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
CRON_SECRET = "<CRON_SECRET>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def setup_teardown():
    resource_id = <seed_resource_no_policy>(BASE_URL)
    yield resource_id
    <cleanup_resource>(BASE_URL, resource_id)

def test_retention_job_raises_incident_when_mandatory_policy_element_missing(setup_teardown):
    resource_id = setup_teardown
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {CRON_SECRET}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)

    body = resp.json()
    policy_errors = body.get("policy_errors", [])
    assert resource_id in policy_errors

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200

    incidents = <get_incidents>(
        BASE_URL, reason="MISSING_MANDATORY_ELEMENT"
    )
    assert any(i["resource_id"] == resource_id for i in incidents)
